items.title: India Ranks 42nd in Global Innovation Index 2025
items.whyInNews: The Global Innovation Index 2025 was released on 15 April 2026, ranking India 42nd out of 132 countries, with Switzerland ranking 1st.
items.summary: The Global Innovation Index 2025 report was published by the World Intellectual Property Organization, ranking India 42nd in terms of innovation, with a score of 37.4 out of 100. The report highlights India's strengths in information and communication technology and its weaknesses in business environment and human capital.
keyPoints: India ranked 42nd out of 132 countries
Switzerland ranked 1st with a score of 64.6
India's score was 37.4 out of 100
importantPoints: Global Innovation Index was first published in 2007
It is published by the World Intellectual Property Organization
India has improved its ranking by 3 places since 2024
The report is based on 81 indicators
items.examRelevance: General Knowledge for SSC CGL, topic - Science and Technology

items.title: RBI Increases Repo Rate to 6.5%
items.whyInNews: The Reserve Bank of India increased the repo rate by 25 basis points to 6.5% on 16 April 2026, to control inflation.
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India, in its monetary policy review, increased the repo rate to 6.5% to control inflation, which is above the target range of 2-6%. The reverse repo rate was also increased to 6.2%. The decision is expected to reduce demand and control prices.
keyPoints: Repo rate increased by 25 basis points
New repo rate is 6.5%
Reverse repo rate is 6.2%
importantPoints: Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends to banks
Reverse repo rate is the rate at which RBI borrows from banks
Inflation target range is 2-6%
Monetary policy is used to control inflation
items.examRelevance: Economics for SSC CGL, topic - Monetary Policy

items.title: India Wins 3 Gold Medals at Asian Athletics Championships
items.whyInNews: India won 3 gold medals at the Asian Athletics Championships held in Bangkok, Thailand from 12-16 April 2026.
items.summary: Indian athletes won 3 gold medals, 2 silver medals, and 4 bronze medals at the Asian Athletics Championships. The gold medals were won by Neeraj Chopra in javelin throw, Seema Punia in discus throw, and the 4x400m relay team.
keyPoints: India won 3 gold medals
Neeraj Chopra won gold in javelin throw
Seema Punia won gold in discus throw
importantPoints: Asian Athletics Championships is held every 2 years
India has won a total of 10 gold medals in the championships
Neeraj Chopra is the current world champion in javelin throw
Seema Punia is a 4-time Asian champion
items.examRelevance: Sports for SSC CGL, topic - Athletics

items.title: Government Launches Scheme to Promote Electric Vehicles
items.whyInNews: The Government of India launched a scheme to promote electric vehicles on 15 April 2026, with a budget of Rs 10,000 crore.
items.summary: The scheme, launched by the Ministry of Heavy Industries, aims to promote the adoption of electric vehicles in the country. The scheme will provide incentives to manufacturers and buyers of electric vehicles, and will also support the development of charging infrastructure.

Scheme aims to promote electric vehicles
Incentives will be provided to manufacturers and buyers
importantPoints: Scheme is part of the National Electric Mobility Mission Plan

Scheme will create jobs in the electric vehicle sector

items.whyInNews: The World Press Freedom Index 2026 was released on 17 April 2026, ranking India 77th out of 180 countries.
items.summary: The World Press Freedom Index 2026 report was published by Reporters Without Borders, ranking India 77th in terms of press freedom. The report highlights the challenges faced by journalists in India, including violence and harassment.

items.title: NASA's Artemis Program to Send First Woman to Moon
items.whyInNews: NASA's Artemis program is scheduled to send the first woman to the moon in 2026, as part of its mission to return humans to the lunar surface.
items.summary: The Artemis program, launched by NASA in 2019, aims to return humans to the lunar surface by 2026. The program will send the first woman to the moon, as well as the first person of color. The mission will also include the establishment of a sustainable presence on the lunar surface.
keyPoints: Artemis program to send first woman to moon
Mission to return humans to lunar surface by 2026
Program will establish sustainable presence on lunar surface
importantPoints: Artemis program was launched in 2019
Program is named after the Greek goddess of the hunt
Mission will include the use of a new lunar lander
Program will also include the establishment of a lunar base
items.examRelevance: Science and Technology for SSC CGL, topic - Space Exploration

items.title: India and US to Hold Joint Military Exercise
items.whyInNews: India and the US will hold a joint military exercise, called Yudh Abhyas, in Alaska, USA from 20-30 April 2026.
items.summary: The exercise, which will be held at the Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson in Alaska, will involve troops from the Indian Army and the US Army. The exercise will focus on counter-terrorism and counter-insurgency operations.
keyPoints: Exercise to be held in Alaska, USA
Exercise will involve troops from Indian Army and US Army
Exercise will focus on counter-terrorism and counter-insurgency operations
importantPoints: Yudh Abhyas is an annual exercise between India and US
Exercise aims to enhance interoperability between the two armies
Exercise will include training on counter-terrorism and counter-insurgency operations
Exercise will also include training on humanitarian assistance and disaster relief
items.examRelevance: Defence for SSC CGL, topic - Military Exercises

items.title: India and EU to Hold Summit on 20 April 2026
items.whyInNews: India and the European Union will hold a summit on 20 April 2026, in Brussels, Belgium.
items.summary: The summit, which will be attended by Prime Minister Narendra Modi and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen, will focus on trade, investment, and climate change. The summit will also discuss the ongoing conflict in Ukraine and its impact on global security.

items.title: India and China to Hold Border Talks on 22 April 2026
items.whyInNews: India and China will hold border talks on 22 April 2026, in New Delhi.
items.summary: The talks, which will be attended by officials from the Ministry of External Affairs and the Chinese Foreign Ministry, will focus on the ongoing border dispute between the two countries. The talks will also discuss the implementation of the agreement on the disengagement of troops from the Line of Actual Control.
keyPoints: Talks to be held on 22 April 2026
Talks will be attended by officials from the Ministry of External Affairs and the Chinese Foreign Ministry
Talks will focus on the ongoing border dispute
